title: cyanus pseudoaxillaris
intro: Cyanus pseudoaxillaris, also known as the Marsh Marigold of New Zealand, is a perennial plant native to the country. It has been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cyanus pseudoaxillaris include its ability to soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and skin regeneration. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
